top_signals.title: Dry Bulk Hormuz transits now at 100% pre-war levels!
top_signals.summary: AXS Marine reported that its AIS-derived data indicates 62 verified transits through the Strait on 24 June, the highest single-day count since the conflict began. This represents 53% of the traffic recorded on the same day last year. The five-day average from 20–24 June now stands at 38.2 daily crossings, compared with 4.5 per day ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Godiva’s Japan Lenders Agree on Nine-Month Loan Extension
top_signals.summary: Lenders to Godiva Japan Inc. have agreed to extend by nine months the maturity of about ¥75 billion ($463 million) in loans to the struggling chocolate maker, people familiar with the matter said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: How the SpaceX IPO revealed a ‘back door’ into Britain’s capital markets
top_signals.summary: The UK’s public offer platform regime was rolled out as part of a package of reforms designed to get capital flowing into British companies. Now SpaceX has used it to raise funding in the biggest IPO of all time – in New York. Maisie Grice asks whether the changes are working as intended. In January, [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Vard Delivers Hybrid Cable Layer to Toyo Construction
top_signals.summary: Norwegian shipbuilder Vard has delivered a hybrid construction and cable laying vessel (CLV) to Japan's Toyo Construction, marking its first vessel delivery to the Japanese offshore wind market.The vessel was handed over at Vard Langsten in Norway on June 26.
